metadata.title: Cyanoacrylate instillation for occlusion of parotid sinuses
metadata.description: Tacit knowledge graph encoding clinical reasoning, procedural expertise, decision logic, complication management, and governance requirements for cyanoacrylate instillation for parotid sinus occlusion.

properties.location: Anterior to and below the ears, overlying the masseter muscle
properties.nerve_supply: parasympathetic via glossopharyngeal nerve (CN IX) via Jacobson's nerve and auriculotemporal nerve
properties.key_relation: Facial nerve (CN VII) divides within the parotid, separating superficial and deep lobes

properties.context: Residual secretory tissue remaining after superficial parotidectomy
properties.relevance: Continues producing saliva that drives sinus symptoms; source of pressure and flow through the sinus tract
properties.tacit: Even small remnants maintain secretory activity; complete removal (deep lobe excision) risks facial nerve damage

properties.location: Deep to facial nerve plane, medial to the retromandibular vein
properties.surgical_access: Requires dissection around facial nerve branches
properties.excision_risk: High risk of facial nerve branch damage during excision

properties.surgical_access: Standard parotidectomy approach; facial nerve identified and preserved

properties.function: Motor control of muscles of facial expression; taste anterior 2/3 tongue; parasympathetic to lacrimal, submandibular, sublingual glands
properties.parotid_course: Exits stylomastoid foramen, enters posterior parotid, divides into superior and inferior divisions within gland
properties.surgical_relevance: Anatomical landmark for parotidectomy; must be identified and preserved
properties.injury_consequence: Facial palsy - partial or complete, temporary or permanent depending on extent of damage

properties.distribution_within_parotid: Fan-shaped branching pattern within parotid substance
properties.mnemonic: Two Zebras Bit My Cat (temporal, zygomatic, buccal, marginal mandibular, cervical)

properties.description: Abnormal epithelialised channel between parotid gland remnant and outer cheek skin
properties.content: Saliva under pressure from ongoing gland secretion
properties.direction: Internal (parotid remnant) to external (cheek skin surface)
properties.mechanism_of_formation: Failed healing of post-parotidectomy dead space / salivary leakage creating epithelialised track
properties.target_of_procedure: Cyanoacrylate solution injected via this tract to seal it

properties.appearance: Visible opening, may discharge saliva or pus
properties.clinical_significance: Access point for injection; indicator of sinus activity
properties.post_treatment_appearance: Becomes 'only just visible' after successful treatment (case report)

properties.definition: Abnormal tract between parotid gland remnants and outer cheek surface, developing after superficial parotid gland surgery
properties.incidence: 10-15% of superficial parotid gland surgery patients
properties.natural_history: May persist indefinitely without intervention; some resolve with conservative measures

properties.definition: Inflammation or infection of the parotid gland
properties.in_context: Complication observed following cyanoacrylate injection in the single published case
clinical_signs: pus leaking from mouth of fistula
properties.severity: Moderate; resolved with antibiotic treatment in the reported case
properties.prevention_method: Prophylactic antibiotic treatment for 3 weeks after injection

properties.definition: Chronic inflammatory condition of salivary gland with recurrent episodes
properties.relevance: One indication for subtotal parotidectomy, establishing the surgical context that may result in parotid sinus
properties.source: Schultz PW 1983 (reference in NICE evidence overview)

properties.route: Through sinus tract to cheek skin surface
properties.functional_impact: Significant - constant wetness, excoriation, social embarrassment
properties.volume_in_case: After first injection: 50% reduction at 7 days; near-complete cessation at 4 months

properties.cause: Repeated exposure of perilesional skin to salivary enzymes and moisture
properties.severity: Can be significant with chronic high-volume leakage
properties.treatment_response: Resolved in case report after successful procedure

properties.nature: Visible opening and associated skin changes on cheek
properties.patient_impact: Significant distress; may affect social function and quality of life
properties.post_treatment: Fistula orifice 'only just visible' after successful treatment in case report

properties.significance: Indicates active infection (parotitis) - requires urgent antibiotic treatment
properties.clinical_action: Distinguish from normal salivary discharge; culture if possible
properties.in_case_report: Observed after second injection before prophylaxis was initiated

properties.rationale: Some sinuses resolve spontaneously; avoids risk of intervention
properties.indication: Recent post-operative period, mild or improving symptoms
properties.limitation: Fails in a proportion of patients who develop chronic symptomatic sinus

properties.mechanism: External compression reduces salivary flow through sinus tract; promotes adhesion of tract walls
properties.limitation: Compliance issues; limited efficacy for established tracts

properties.mechanism: Reduces salivary gland secretory function by damaging acinar cells
properties.tacit: Rarely used in modern practice for this indication due to disproportionate risk profile
properties.note: Listed as option in NICE guidance; clinical context suggests it would be a later-line option

properties.mechanism: Interrupts parasympathetic nerve supply to parotid (via Jacobson's nerve / lesser petrosal nerve), reducing secretion
properties.approach: Middle ear surgical approach (tympanic neurectomy) or via auriculotemporal nerve
properties.tacit: Secretory function may recover over time as nerves regenerate; effect may not be permanent

nodes.label: Excision of deep lobe of parotid gland

properties.mechanism: Removes residual secretory tissue, eliminating source of saliva driving the sinus
properties.facial_nerve_risk_detail: Facial nerve passes through parotid gland; branches must be dissected free of deep lobe tissue, risking traction injury, transection, or thermal damage
properties.expected_outcomes: High efficacy but significant morbidity risk
properties.tacit: This procedure's facial nerve risk is the primary clinical reason why cyanoacrylate instillation, despite its minimal evidence base, may be preferred

nodes.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation for parotid sinus occlusion

properties.route: sinus tract into parotid gland remnant
properties.mechanism: Cyanoacrylate polymerises on contact with tissue fluid, creating adhesive occlusion of the sinus tract and gland remnant
properties.repeat_indication: If procedure unsuccessful and symptoms recur
properties.nice_recommendation_basis: evidence does not appear adequate for routine use without special arrangements
properties.developer: Mr AJ Marcus, Edgware Hospital, Middlesex, UK
properties.tacit: Minimally invasive alternative that may avoid the facial nerve risk of deep lobe excision; clinical preference may favour this despite weak evidence precisely because the surgical alternative carries high-stakes irreversible morbidity

properties.purpose: Removal of superficial lobe of parotid gland
indications: lymph node disease in parotid region
properties.technique: Facial nerve identification and preservation, dissection of superficial lobe
properties.relevance_to_graph: Causal antecedent of parotid sinus; this is the procedure that creates the condition requiring treatment

properties.indication: Established parotitis or infection following cyanoacrylate injection
properties.route: systemic (oral or IV depending on severity)
properties.organisms_likely: Mixed oral flora; Staphylococcus aureus; anaerobes
properties.tacit: Antibiotic type and duration not specified in case report; empirical broad-spectrum choice reasonable

properties.indication: Administration after cyanoacrylate injection to prevent parotitis
properties.timing: After second injection (empirically adopted after parotitis following first injection without prophylaxis)
properties.tacit: The 3-week duration is entirely empirical from one practitioner's experience; it is not evidence-based but represents the only available clinical guidance on prevention. This knowledge exists only through contact with the originating practitioner.

properties.mechanism: Polymerises rapidly on contact with nucleophiles (tissue fluid, blood); forms strong covalent bonds with tissue
properties.tacit: The analogical reasoning from cyanoacrylate's established use in sealing vascular abnormal tracts (AVF, PDA) is the theoretical basis for its use in parotid sinus, even though the tissue context differs significantly

properties.type: iodised ethyl ester of fatty acids (contrast agent and carrier)
properties.role_in_mixture: Carrier vehicle for cyanoacrylate; slows polymerisation to allow injection
properties.independent_use: Used in interventional radiology for chemoembolisation, lymphangiography
properties.tacit: The lipiodol-cyanoacrylate ratio is not specified in the guidance; this is a critical technical detail held only by the originating practitioner

properties.setting_time: Rapid polymerisation on contact with tissue fluid
properties.tacit: The precise mixing ratio, volume injected, and injection technique are unspecified procedural details that constitute core tacit knowledge accessible only from the originating practitioner

properties.incidence_in_evidence: 1/1 patient in case report (occurred after first injection, before prophylaxis initiated)
properties.presentation: Pus leaking from mouth of fistula
properties.recurrence_in_case: Did not recur after prophylactic antibiotics were used for second injection
properties.note: Frequency in wider population unknown due to n=1 evidence

properties.context: Risk of deep lobe excision surgery, NOT of cyanoacrylate instillation
properties.mechanism: Traction, transection, or thermal injury during surgical dissection of deep parotid lobe
properties.tacit: This is the primary risk that creates clinical equipoise between doing nothing and doing surgery; it is the key decision-weight that makes cyanoacrylate's uncertain benefit potentially worthwhile

properties.reason: Insufficient evidence (n=1 case report) to characterise complication profile
properties.regulatory_implication: Primary reason for NICE 'special arrangements' requirement
properties.tacit: The absence of known complications is not the same as absence of complications; the unknowns here are the dominant clinical risk, not the known parotitis

properties.context: Risk of repeat parotitis with subsequent injections if prophylaxis not used
properties.prevention: Prophylactic antibiotics for 3 weeks after each injection
properties.evidence_basis: Single case - first injection without prophylaxis caused parotitis; second injection with 3-week prophylaxis prevented recurrence

properties.title: The treatment of a chronic parotid cutaneous fistula by the injection of a solution of lipiodiol with cyanoacrylate
properties.validity: Demonstrates feasibility only; lacks power for any statistical inference
properties.generalisability: None - single patient, single practitioner, single institution

properties.title: Post-parotidectomy fistula
properties.relevance: Epidemiological reference establishing 10-15% incidence of parotid sinus after superficial parotidectomy

properties.title: Subtotal parotidectomy in the treatment of chronic sialadenitis
properties.relevance: Context reference for surgical management of parotid conditions that may result in post-operative sinus

properties.author: Bazian Ltd
properties.type: rapid literature review and specialist advisory review
properties.conclusion: No controlled studies or case series found; one case report only; feasibility demonstrated but insufficient evidence for routine use

properties.document_type: NICE HealthTech guidance (formerly Interventional Procedures Guidance)
properties.status: Current - no review date set (pending new evidence)

properties.metric: 50% reduction in saliva secretion through sinus
properties.interpretation: Partial response at early timepoint; warrants continued observation rather than re-injection or escalation
Treatment success; expected progressive improvement over months rather than days

properties.metric: Excoriation around mouth of sinus resolved
properties.interpretation: Secondary benefit from reduction in salivary irritation of perilesional skin
Significant cosmetic improvement; may be primary goal for some patients

properties.measurement: Subjective patient report - no validated PRO instrument
properties.limitation: Single patient, no comparator, no validated scale

properties.nice_finding: Current evidence on safety and efficacy does NOT appear adequate for procedure to be used without special arrangements
properties.basis: Single uncontrolled case report (n=1); no specialist advisor aware of technique
properties.consequence: Special arrangements for consent and audit required before proceeding
properties.trigger_for_upgrade: Publication of further safety and efficacy outcomes
properties.tacit: This is not a permanent bar to the procedure; it is an epistemic state that can change with evidence. The clinician must understand that 'special arrangements' is a governance status, not a prohibition.

nodes.label: Facial nerve risk of surgical alternative

properties.description: The risk of facial nerve branch damage from deep lobe excision creates clinical rationale for attempting minimally invasive occlusion despite weak evidence
properties.weight: High - permanent facial palsy is an irreversible, high-impact outcome
properties.tacit: This is the core tacit decision logic that makes cyanoacrylate instillation clinically rational even with n=1 evidence: the downside of the alternative is catastrophic and irreversible, while cyanoacrylate's downside appears manageable and reversible based on the single case.

properties.description: If the procedure is unsuccessful and symptoms recur, the procedure can be repeated
properties.decision_logic: Partial or no response at first injection → repeat rather than escalate to surgery
properties.number_of_repeats_in_evidence: 1 repeat (2 total injections) in case report
properties.tacit: The response timeline is weeks to months (not days). Early partial response at 7 days is a positive prognostic sign; escalation should not occur before adequate time has been allowed. This sequential assessment is invisible in the guidance text.

properties.description: Symptom persistence or recurrence after a treatment tier is the trigger for escalating to the next tier
properties.applies_to: All treatment tiers in the management ladder
properties.tacit: The duration of watchful waiting before escalation is unspecified; clinical judgement about patient distress, cosmetic impact, and functional limitation governs timing

properties.action: Inform clinical governance leads in the Trust
properties.purpose: Ensure appropriate institutional oversight for a procedure with inadequate evidence

properties.requirement: Patient must understand uncertainty about procedure safety and efficacy
properties.format: Clear written information must be provided
properties.standard: Higher than standard consent - specific acknowledgement of uncertainty required
properties.tacit: Standard surgical consent covers known risks; this special consent is specifically about epistemic uncertainty - the patient must understand that NO ONE knows the full risk profile because of the n=1 evidence base

properties.requirement: Audit and review clinical outcomes of all patients
properties.reporting: Publication of safety and efficacy outcomes recommended
properties.note: All cases should be captured - no minimum threshold

properties.purpose: Reduce current uncertainty about safety and efficacy
properties.tacit: Publication of even small case series would represent a meaningful evidence upgrade from n=1 single case

properties.condition: NICE may review the procedure upon publication of further evidence
properties.not_time_bound: No scheduled review date; contingent on new evidence only
properties.implication: Guidance status is indefinitely 'special arrangements' unless new evidence published
properties.tacit: In practice, this procedure may remain at 'special arrangements' tier indefinitely if no evidence accumulates

properties.name: Mr AJ Marcus
properties.institution: Edgware Hospital, London (also cited as Middlesex, UK)
properties.role: Sole practitioner of this technique at time of guidance
properties.knowledge_status: Holds all tacit procedural knowledge for this technique
properties.nice_recommendation: Anyone considering use of this procedure may wish to contact this clinician
properties.tacit: The totality of procedural expertise for this intervention resides with one person. Training pathway = direct contact. This is an extreme case of tacit knowledge concentration.

properties.fact: Procedure performed once, on 1 patient, by 1 clinician, in 1 country at time of guidance
properties.implication_for_training: No established training pathway; no simulation models; no multi-centre experience
properties.implication_for_evidence: Randomised trial would require impractical numbers given rarity of indication
properties.implication_for_governance: Special arrangements are not bureaucratic formality - they reflect genuine epistemic limitation
properties.tacit: The request to SERNIP (Safety and Efficacy Register for New Interventional Procedures) originated with the same clinician who performed the only case - the procedure was self-nominated for review

properties.principle: Cyanoacrylate is well-established for sealing abnormal tracts in other contexts
properties.tacit: The theoretical rationale for parotid sinus occlusion is borrowed from vascular applications; however, the tissue environment, injection volumes, setting times, and complication profiles differ substantially. The analogy provides a rational basis but not a proven extrapolation.

edges.label: Superficial parotidectomy may result in parotid sinus

properties.mechanism: Failed wound healing creates epithelialised tract between gland remnant and skin

edges.label: Superficial parotidectomy leaves parotid gland remnant

properties.reason: Deep lobe not removed in superficial procedure
properties.significance: Remnant continues secreting saliva, driving sinus flow

edges.label: Gland remnant communicates with cheek via sinus tract
Sinus tract opens at fistula orifice on cheek
Parotid sinus causes chronic saliva leakage

properties.mechanism: Saliva travels through sinus tract to skin surface

edges.label: Parotid sinus causes cheek excoriation

properties.mechanism: Chronic salivary contact with perilesional skin causes enzymatic and moisture damage

edges.label: Parotid sinus causes cosmetic disfigurement

properties.mechanism: Visible fistula orifice and associated skin changes on cheek

edges.label: Parotid sinus may be managed initially with watchful waiting

properties.conditions: mild or early symptoms; post-operative period where spontaneous resolution possible

edges.label: Parotid sinus may be managed with bandaging

properties.conditions: failed watchful waiting or concurrent with watchful waiting

edges.label: Parotid sinus may be managed with radiotherapy

properties.caveat: Rarely used in modern practice due to disproportionate risk profile

edges.label: Parotid sinus may be managed with local denervation

properties.conditions: failed conservative measures; preference for avoiding deep surgery

edges.label: Parotid sinus may be managed with deep lobe excision

properties.conditions: failed all other measures; significant disability

edges.label: Parotid sinus is the indication for cyanoacrylate instillation
Failed watchful waiting may escalate to bandaging

properties.tacit_note: Duration of observation before escalation is unspecified; clinician judgement on symptom burden governs timing

edges.label: Failed bandaging may escalate to radiotherapy

properties.tacit_note: In practice, denervation or cyanoacrylate may be preferred over radiotherapy due to risk profile

edges.label: Failed bandaging may escalate to local denervation

properties.trigger: Persistent symptoms; preference for surgical over radiation

edges.label: Failed bandaging may lead to consideration of cyanoacrylate instillation

properties.trigger: Persistent symptoms; desire to avoid surgery
properties.tacit_note: Under special arrangements; requires governance notification and special consent before proceeding

edges.label: Failed denervation may escalate to deep lobe excision

properties.trigger: Persistent symptoms; secretion not adequately reduced by denervation

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation can be repeated if unsuccessful

properties.number_in_evidence: 1 repeat (2 total injections) in case report
properties.tacit_note: Partial response at 7 days is normal progression; full assessment should allow weeks to months before declaring failure

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation is an alternative to deep lobe excision

properties.advantage: Avoids facial nerve risk of surgery
properties.decision_weight: Facial nerve risk creates strong clinical rationale for attempting less invasive option first
properties.tacit_note: This is the central clinical equipoise: the minimally invasive option has minimal evidence but avoids a catastrophic-risk surgical option. The uncertainty of cyanoacrylate must be weighed against the known high-stakes risk of the alternative.

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation avoids the facial nerve risk of deep lobe excision

properties.mechanism: Minimally invasive injection route does not require facial nerve dissection
properties.clinical_significance: Primary driver of clinical interest in procedure despite weak evidence base
properties.tacit_note: An experienced clinician weighs unquantified cyanoacrylate risks against well-known facial palsy risk of surgery

edges.label: Deep lobe excision risks facial nerve branch damage

properties.mechanism: Facial nerve passes through parotid gland; branches must be dissected free of deep lobe
properties.tacit_note: Risk level depends heavily on surgeon experience and residual anatomy from prior surgery; not quantified in guidance

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation uses lipiodol-cyanoacrylate solution
Solution contains cyanoacrylate
Solution contains lipiodol

properties.role: carrier vehicle; slows cyanoacrylate polymerisation to allow injection

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate solution injected via sinus tract

properties.route: injection through fistula orifice into sinus tract

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation targets parotid gland remnant

properties.mechanism: Solution injected into remnant tissue, sealing internal sinus origin

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation risks parotitis

properties.incidence: 1/1 in case report (100% without prophylaxis; 0/1 with prophylaxis)
properties.note: Population incidence entirely unknown due to n=1 evidence

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation has uncharacterised risk of unknown complications

properties.reason: n=1 evidence base; no controlled studies; no case series
properties.regulatory_consequence: Special arrangements required precisely because complication profile is unknown

edges.label: Parotitis managed with antibiotic treatment
Prophylactic antibiotics prevent recurrent parotitis

properties.evidence: Single case - parotitis after injection without prophylaxis; no parotitis after injection with 3-week prophylaxis
properties.tacit_note: This 3-week protocol is the only available procedural knowledge on prevention. It is empirical, from a single practitioner, but represents the only available clinical guidance.

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate injection should be accompanied by prophylactic antibiotics

properties.basis: Empirical from case report - prevents parotitis
properties.tacit_note: Not stated explicitly in NICE guidance but derivable from the case report data; would be standard practice for any clinician following the originating practitioner's protocol

edges.label: Parotitis manifests as pus discharge from fistula

properties.sign: Pus leaking from mouth of fistula

edges.label: Marcus 1995 case report demonstrates feasibility of the procedure

properties.limitation: Cannot support inferences about efficacy or safety in general population

edges.label: Case report documents 50% secretion reduction at 7 days
Case report documents near-complete secretion cessation at 4 months
Case report documents excoriation resolution
Case report documents near-invisible fistula at 4 months
Case report documents patient satisfaction
Case report documents parotitis after first injection
Wax & Tarshis 1991 establishes 10-15% incidence of parotid sinus
IPAC overview identified single case report as sole evidence

properties.finding: No controlled studies or case series found

edges.label: Evidence inadequacy requires governance notification before proceeding
Evidence inadequacy requires special informed consent
Evidence inadequacy requires clinical audit of all patients
Clinical audit enables outcome publication

properties.purpose: Reduce uncertainty through published evidence accumulation

edges.label: Publication of outcomes may trigger NICE guidance review

properties.nice_statement: NICE may review the procedure upon publication of further evidence

edges.label: NICE HTG20 mandates clinical governance notification
NICE HTG20 mandates special informed consent
NICE HTG20 mandates clinical audit
Mr AJ Marcus authored the sole case report
Mr AJ Marcus is the sole known practitioner of this technique

properties.nice_recommendation: Anyone considering use may wish to contact this clinician
properties.tacit_note: Procedural expertise is held entirely by this practitioner; there is no other training source

edges.label: Extreme procedure rarity explains evidence inadequacy

properties.logic: 1 patient globally → no studies possible → NICE must apply special arrangements

edges.label: Extreme rarity concentrates all procedural knowledge in one practitioner

properties.tacit_note: Maximum concentration of tacit knowledge; no distributed expertise; training requires direct apprenticeship

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate has established use in sealing other abnormal tracts

properties.relevance: Theoretical basis for parotid sinus application
properties.tacit_note: Analogical reasoning from vascular sealing provides scientific plausibility but different tissue environments mean direct extrapolation is uncertain

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate's established sealing uses provide theoretical rationale for parotid application

properties.strength: Moderate - principle sound, tissue context different

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation treats saliva leakage

properties.mechanism: Sinus occlusion eliminates path for saliva to reach skin surface

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation treats cheek excoriation

properties.mechanism: Elimination of salivary contact with skin resolves excoriation

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation treats cosmetic disfigurement

properties.outcome_in_case: Fistula orifice 'only just visible' at 4 months

edges.label: Partial response at 7 days informs repeat injection decision

properties.tacit_note: 50% reduction at day 7 is encouraging; full assessment requires months. Clinician must resist premature escalation. The case report patient received a second injection and reached near-complete cessation.

edges.label: Facial nerve risk criterion discourages deep lobe excision

properties.logic: Risk of irreversible facial palsy shifts risk-benefit calculation towards less invasive options
properties.tacit_note: Degree of discouragement depends on severity of symptoms; mild symptoms + high surgical risk = strong preference for non-surgical approach

edges.label: Facial nerve risk criterion supports considering cyanoacrylate despite weak evidence

properties.tacit_note: The risk-benefit calculus is not just about cyanoacrylate's evidence; it's about the comparative risk of alternatives. Weak evidence for a low-risk procedure may be preferred over strong evidence for a high-risk procedure when alternative downside is severe.

edges.label: Symptom persistence triggers escalation from watchful waiting
Symptom persistence triggers escalation from bandaging
Cyanoacrylate instillation is a treatment for parotid sinus
Deep lobe excision requires dissection around facial nerve branches

properties.risk: Traction, thermal, or direct injury to branches during dissection
properties.tacit_note: Risk is elevated in revision surgery due to scarring from prior superficial parotidectomy

edges.label: Facial nerve divides into peripheral branches within parotid gland

properties.division_point: Within parotid substance after exiting stylomastoid foramen

edges.label: Facial nerve branches pass through deep parotid lobe territory

properties.clinical_significance: Branches must be identified and preserved during deep lobe surgery

edges.label: Superficial parotidectomy used to treat chronic sialadenitis
Schultz 1983 documents subtotal parotidectomy for sialadenitis

properties.context: Background reference establishing surgical context leading to parotid sinus

edges.label: IPAC overview confirms extreme rarity of procedure

properties.finding: Author of case report unaware of any other practitioners performing procedure; has not performed on any other patients

edges.label: IPAC overview supports NICE finding of evidence inadequacy

properties.finding: No controlled studies or case series identified; single uncontrolled case report only

edges.label: Repeat cyanoacrylate injections carry recurrent infection risk if prophylaxis omitted

properties.tacit_note: Each injection episode creates an infection window; prophylaxis must be repeated with each injection episode

edges.label: Recurrent infection risk managed by prophylactic antibiotics with each injection
Parotid gland contains superficial lobe
Parotid gland contains deep lobe
Superficial parotidectomy removes superficial lobe
Deep lobe excision removes deep lobe
Local denervation reduces parotid secretion

properties.mechanism: Interrupts parasympathetic supply via Jacobson's nerve / lesser petrosal nerve
properties.durability: May not be permanent due to nerve regeneration

edges.label: Radiotherapy reduces parotid secretion

properties.mechanism: Radiation damage to acinar cells reduces secretory capacity

edges.label: Bandaging applies compression to reduce flow through sinus tract

properties.mechanism: External pressure collapses or reduces calibre of superficial tract

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation occludes the sinus tract

properties.mechanism: Polymerised cyanoacrylate physically seals the tract lumen

edges.label: NICE HTG20 recommends publication of outcomes
NICE HTG20 directs interested clinicians to contact Mr AJ Marcus
Evidence inadequacy restricts cyanoacrylate instillation to special arrangements context

properties.restriction: Cannot be used as routine procedure without special arrangements
properties.tacit_note: Special arrangements is a governance status, not a prohibition. Procedure can be offered to patients where clinically appropriate, provided all NICE requirements are met.

edges.label: Secretion cessation outcome resolves saliva leakage symptom
Excoriation resolution outcome resolves cheek excoriation symptom
Near-invisible fistula partially resolves cosmetic disfigurement

properties.note: Fistula 'only just visible' - cosmetically improved but not entirely invisible

edges.label: Repeatability of procedure modulates when symptom persistence triggers escalation

properties.tacit_note: Symptom persistence does not necessarily trigger escalation to surgery; first step is to consider repeat injection. Escalation to surgery should occur only after repeat injection failure.

edges.label: Cyanoacrylate instillation produced patient satisfaction in the single case

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Risk-benefit inversion
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: The primary reason cyanoacrylate instillation is considered despite n=1 evidence is that the surgical alternative (deep lobe excision) carries an irreversible, high-stakes risk (facial palsy) that may outweigh the quantified benefits of surgery. Weak evidence for a low-risk procedure may be clinically preferred over strong evidence for a high-risk one.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Prophylaxis protocol derivation
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: The 3-week post-injection antibiotic prophylaxis protocol is empirical knowledge held exclusively by the originating practitioner. It is not stated in NICE guidance but is derivable from the case report. It must be adopted because parotitis occurred without it and did not recur with it.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Temporal assessment of response
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: Partial response (50% reduction) at 7 days is expected and not a failure indication. Full assessment requires months. Early escalation to surgery based on 7-day partial response would be inappropriate. The clinician must allow adequate time before declaring failure.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Knowledge concentration and transfer
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: All procedural tacit knowledge for this technique is concentrated in one practitioner. Critical unknowns include: lipiodol:cyanoacrylate ratio, injection volume, needle gauge, patient positioning, and technique for confirming sinus anatomy before injection. Training can only occur through direct contact with Mr AJ Marcus.

key_clinical_reasoning_chains.name: Epistemic vs procedural governance
key_clinical_reasoning_chains.description: Special arrangements are not imposed because the procedure is known to be dangerous, but because its safety profile is unknown. The governance requirements (consent, notification, audit) are epistemic responses to uncertainty, not risk responses to known harm. Clinicians must understand this distinction when explaining special arrangements to patients.
